title: Paul Thomas Anderson Wins Oscar for Best Adapted Screenplay
text: Paul Thomas Anderson has finally achieved Academy Award recognition after three decades as a celebrated American filmmaker. He won the Oscar for Best Adapted Screenplay for One Battle After Another, marking his first win following eleven previous nominations. Anderson's screenplay adaptation represents a significant career milestone. In his acceptance remarks, he briefly referenced Thomas Pynchon before addressing his family. Anderson expressed the film's personal significance: I wrote this movie for my kids, to say sorry for the housekeeping mess that we left in this world while encouraging the next generation toward common sense and decency. The screenplay adapts sections from Thomas Pynchon's 1990 novel Vineland, which followed Gravity's Rainbow. Anderson substantially transformed the material, changing character names, the time period, and removing decades of subplot material. This marks his second Pynchon adaptation—he previously received an Adapted Screenplay nomination for Inherent Vice in 2014. One Battle After Another earned thirteen total Oscar nominations, including Best Picture, Best Director, Best Actor (Leonardo DiCaprio), and multiple technical categories.
